title: tithonia calva var. calva
intro: Tithonia calva var. calva, also known as the Mexican marigold, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and parts of Central America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of terpenoids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Tithonia calva var. calva include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other medicinal plants, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
